_comment: PRIVACY GATE for the Obsidian projection. ONLY items listed here are projected, and ONLY the metadata fields below — never note bodies, never private notes. This file and the generated data/projections/obsidian.json are committed to a PUBLIC repo, so everything here must be public-safe. Review before regenerating. vaultKey = the note's filename (lowercased, no extension).

concepts.title: Authority Architecture
concepts.summary: Governance for AI-native systems is an architecture problem: authority has to be designed, placed, and made legible — not assumed.

concepts.title: Agency Stack
concepts.summary: Agency in AI systems is layered; naming the stack shows where human judgment must sit.

concepts.title: Escalation Architecture
concepts.summary: Escalation routes a decision to the right level of authority instead of automating everything equally.

concepts.title: Governance Debt
concepts.summary: The deferred cost of automations no one owns — it compounds quietly, like technical debt.

concepts.title: Design Systems Becoming Decision Systems
concepts.summary: Design systems stopped being component libraries and became systems that make decisions.

concepts.title: Authority Collapse
concepts.summary: The failure mode where authority quietly drains out of a system until no one can say who decided.

concepts.title: Human Authority Layer
concepts.summary: The designed place in a system where a human retains and exercises decision authority.

concepts.title: Governance Layer
concepts.summary: Where a system's decisions are checked, recorded, and owned.
